zh.lifeSummary: 當William Downes I於1288年出生在英國爾塞頓時，他的父親Robert de Downes50歲，母親Margaret Fitton11285歲。他於從1313年1月9日到1314年1月8日與Maude de Shrigley結婚。他們至少是7個兒子的父母。他去世於1329年之後在英國爾波特 史利格利。

nameMeanings.name: Downes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Shropshire and Staffordshire): variant of Down with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish (counties Clare and Limerick): shortened Anglicized form of Irish Gaelic Ó Dubháin (see Doane ).

variants.name: Down
meanings: English (Devon): topographic name for someone who lived on or near a down or low hill, from Middle English dun(e), doun(e) (Old English dūn) ‘down, low hill’. See also Downer .

English (Devon): possibly in some cases a patronymic from the Old English personal name Dūn, but later names of this origin cannot reliably be distinguished from those originating from the topographic sense. See Dunn 2.

variants.name: Downs
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Downes  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When William Downes I was born in 1288, in Sutton, Cheshire, England, his father, Robert de Downes, was 50 and his mother, Margaret Fitton, was 11285. He married Maude de Shrigley from 9 January 1313 to 8 January 1314. They were the parents of at least 7 sons. He died after 1329, in Pott Shrigley, Cheshire, England.

Il a épousé Maude de Shrigley du 9 janvier 1313 au 8 janvier 1314. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils. Il est décédé après 1329, à Pott Shrigley, Cheshire, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: William Downes I(이)가 1288년 영국 체실르 수턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Downes(은)는 50세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Fitton(은)는 11285세였다. 그는 1313년 1월 9일부터 1314년 1월 8일까지 Maude de Shrigley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1329년 이후 영국 체실르 포트 슈리글리에서 사망했다.
